title: Fallbacks and Graceful Degrades
concept: If the model fails or times out, the application should display a helpful, pre-configured fallback message rather than a raw code error.

exercises.prompt: If an AI tool goes offline, what is the best way to handle the situation?

options.text: Show a helpful backup message instead of a crash screen
Make the user restart their device
Force the AI to work faster by spamming requests

exercises.explain: When things go wrong, we want our application to degrade 'gracefully'—meaning it handles the failure elegantly without completely breaking for the user.

cards.title: When AI Takes a Nap
cards.body: What happens when an AI model crashes or loses internet? Without a backup plan, users get ugly, scary red error codes. Let's learn how to fail with style using 'Graceful Degrades'!

cards.title: What is a Fallback?
cards.body: A fallback is a backup plan. In AI design, it means having a pre-written, friendly response ready to display if the AI model takes too long, crashes, or goes offline. This ensures your app stays polite and usable, rather than breaking.

cards.title: The Restaurant Menu Backup
cards.body: Think of a restaurant menu. If they run out of the chef's special fancy soup (our AI model), the waiter doesn't scream an error code at you. Instead, they gracefully offer you a delicious house salad (our pre-written fallback). The dinner continues smoothly!

cards.title: A Friendly Plan B
cards.body: Imagine an AI chatbot that recommends movies. If the AI server times out, the code catches the error and instantly displays a pre-saved message: 'Oops! Our film guide is taking a quick nap. In the meantime, we highly recommend checking out some classic family comedies!'

cards.title: Myth: Perfect Apps Never Fail
cards.body: Many think that good apps never experience errors. In reality, even the biggest websites crash! The secret isn't being perfect; it's having excellent fallback plans so users barely notice when something goes wrong.

cards.title: Ugly Crash vs. Graceful Save
cards.body: Let's compare what happens when an AI service fails without a fallback versus with a graceful fallback.

left.text: User sees 'Error 500: API_Connection_Refused' on a blank white screen.

right.text: User sees 'Our AI is resting! Here are some popular activities you can try instead.'

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to show a fallback message when an AI system fails?

options.text: It makes the AI model process information much faster.
It hides ugly technical errors and keeps the user experience friendly.
It automatically fixes the internet connection of the user.
It trains the AI to stop making mistakes.

exercises.explain: Raw programming errors look scary and confusing to everyday users. Fallbacks replace them with helpful, clean messages.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Graceful degradation means keeping the app running with limited features instead of letting it completely crash.
exercises.explain: Graceful degradation means the application still works at a basic, friendly level even when the advanced AI parts stop working.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the design principle:
exercises.sentence: To prevent users from seeing raw code errors, always display a pre-written fallback message.
exercises.explain: A fallback message keeps your app clean and user-friendly, replacing scary computer errors with a helpful backup note.

exercises.prompt: Which of these represents a friendly, graceful fallback response?

options.text: "Our assistant is extra busy right now. Please try again in a few moments!"
[A blank screen that freezes and does not respond to clicks]

exercises.explain: A simple, friendly message that acknowledges the delay is a classic fallback. The other two options are either a raw code error or a complete application crash.

exercises.prompt: Your app uses an AI model to generate personalized bedtime stories, but the AI server goes down. What is the best way to handle this error for a kid using the app?

options.text: Show the raw, detailed system crash report so they can debug it.
Show a friendly, pre-written answer and log the actual error privately.
Freeze the screen and wait forever for the AI to wake up.
Disable the entire application and force the user to reinstall it.

exercises.explain: Always prepare a backup plan! By using standard messages for errors, you prevent your users from seeing confusing technical codes, keeping the experience friendly and professional.
